title: Control job creation with rules, not only/except
description: Express when a job runs using rules with explicit if/changes/when, and avoid mixing the legacy only/except syntax.
body: Use when deciding when a job should be added to a pipeline. Do not mix rules with only/except in the same job (they are mutually exclusive and the combination is rejected or behaves surprisingly) and do not rely on implicit defaults that silently drop a job. Actions: define rules with explicit if conditions (branch, tag, MR, pipeline source), use changes for path-based triggering, and set when: never branches deliberately. Validation signals include the job appearing only in the intended pipeline contexts and a dry view of the pipeline matching expectation. Risk boundary: a rule that runs a deploy job on an unintended ref is a production-reach change; review the conditions before merge.
